title: Given sample log events, identify the likely failing service
description: Read a short set of log events and identify the most likely failing service with your reasoning.
prompt:
Logs:
10:00 web INFO request /checkout
10:00 api INFO checkout started
10:01 payments ERROR timeout calling provider
10:01 api ERROR checkout failed: payment timeout
10:01 web ERROR checkout returned 502

Which service is most likely failing first, and what would you check next?

examples.input: A sequence of web, api, and payments logs
examples.output: Identify payments as likely first failure and suggest next checks.

strong_answer_includes: Payments is the likely first failing service
API and web errors appear downstream
Check payment provider latency, network, retries, and dependency health

sample_answer: The payments service is the most likely first failure because it logs a timeout before the API logs checkout failed and before the web layer returns 502. The API and web errors look like downstream symptoms of the payment timeout. I would check traces or correlation IDs for the checkout request, payment provider health, outbound network rules, retry behavior, and recent changes in the payments service. I would keep some uncertainty because one log sample is not proof, but it is the best first place to investigate.

hint_plan.level_2: Separate root error from propagated errors.

canonical_solution_summary: Payments likely fails first; API and web report downstream errors.
